hypothesis: A generative model trained to jointly predict both the crystal structure and its space group symmetry directly from XRD patterns—using symmetry classification as an auxiliary objective during training rather than as a post-hoc constraint—will produce candidate structures that, when subsequently relaxed with a machine learning interatomic potential, converge to the correct experimental phase at significantly higher rates than models conditioned on XRD alone, because the embedded symmetry inductive bias will steer generated candidates into the basin of attraction of the target energy minimum rather than competing polymorphic minima.
